comments.1752366050091:
Correct parts are not always provided — be prepared to improvise while maintaining quality.

If parts are missing:

Notify the program team immediately.
Document missing items (photo or written note).
Use approved substitutes only — confirm with QC lead if unsure.

Improvisation rules:

Maintain appearance and functionality.
Explain why and how in nightly QC notes.

Cable management standards:

Cables must be routed high and out of the way.
White Velcro only — no zip ties permitted.
Use swivel J-hooks or approved guides where applicable.
No hard bends — maintain service loops where required.

QC expectations:

Inspect cable runs before sensors are finalized.
Take at least one photo of clean, compliant cabling.
Address and correct poor routing immediately.
Record all improvisations or material issues in QC notes.

1774928161197.customerAddress: 1168 E 2100 S, Salt Lake City, UT 84106
1774928161197.notes:
The team demonstrates strong foundational capability in the field. They are quick to learn, communicate effectively, and consistently ask questions when clarification is needed. Their willingness to accept and apply feedback is a clear strength and supports continued development.

Areas for improvement include overall aesthetic quality, precision in installation and attention to detail, particularly during cleanup and site closeout. These gaps appear to be minor and primarily related to project experience rather than skill.

During the visit, I worked directly with the team to address these issues. This included guiding improvements to cable pathway presentation, correcting sensor pole placement, and reinforcing proper measurement techniques by clarifying wall reference points and cross-referencing heat maps with blueprints. I also provided both a tutorial video and a live demonstration on sensor alignment to reinforce expectations.

Overall, the team performs well and shows solid technical understanding of cabling and hardware installation. With continued attention to detail and refinement of installation standards, they have strong potential to develop into a highly effective team.

1776181266350.notes:
Night 1:
Documented inventory & reported crash kit was missing. Assembled and mounted network cabinet. Completed marking sensor locations with masking tape. Completed assembly of clinton poles. Created sleeve penetration. Completed all j-hook pathways. Ran 7 cables and stringline through our main trunk.

Night 2:
Technican Daniel Juarez called out. Ehren Acosta (Tel-Aid) filled in. Completed majority of cable runs. Installed two unistrut for sensor locations. Installed 9 sensors and 9 sensor poles. Completed termination on device end for 9 sensors. Began brining cable trunk into the network rack and started dressing.

end_shift.notes: Scissor lifts are not able to be parked inside. Parked lifts in their original drop-off location.
